question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST endpoint introduce a slow-read attack by sending the HTTP response headers immediately but streaming the body at 1 byte per second, causing `json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode()` in `fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()` to block for the full `cfg.mirrorNodeClientTimeout` duration per attempt, multiplied by all retry attempts, blocking pinger startup for an extended period?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST endpoint return a JSON response where `nodes` is a JSON array containing `null` entries, causing `fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()` to process null node entries and potentially panic or produce unexpected behavior when accessing `n.NodeAccountID` on a zero-value `nodeEntry` struct?
